hawkish_phrases: inflation was expected to pick up towards the end of the year and to increase gradually thereafter
domestic cost pressures and wages were strengthening
high levels of capacity utilisation, labour market tightness and rising wage growth
risks related to global factors, including the threat of increased protectionism, had become more prominent
dovish_phrases: expected the key ECB interest rates to remain at their present levels at least through the summer of 2019
significant monetary policy stimulus was still needed
monetary policy had to remain patient, prudent and persistent
measures of underlying inflation remained generally muted
downside risks to the short-term growth outlook
summary: The Governing Council unanimously held rates at 0.00% (main refinancing) and announced a taper path for asset purchases (€30bn through September, €15bn through December, then end), while introducing enhanced forward guidance that rates would remain at present levels at least through summer 2019, reflecting substantial progress on inflation convergence but emphasizing the need for continued patience and significant ongoing stimulus.
